text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Egg consumption as part of an energy-restricted high-protein diet improves blood lipid and blood glucose profiles in individuals with type 2 diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"- 124.Pearce K.L., Clifton P.M., Noakes M. Egg consumption as part of an energy-restricted high-protein diet improves blood lipid and blood glucose profiles in individuals with type 2 diabetes. Br. J. Nutr. 2011;105:584–592. doi: 10.1017/S0007114510003983.","entailment":"The quoted citation explicitly states that egg consumption within that diet improves blood lipid and glucose profiles in people with type 2 diabetes, directly supporting the claim.","population":"individuals with type 2 diabetes","intervention":"egg consumption as part of an energy-restricted high-protein diet","comparator":"","outcome":"blood lipid and blood glucose profiles","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Egg consumption in infants is associated with longer recumbent length and greater intake of several nutrients essential in growth and development.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"- 128.Papanikolaou Y., Fulgoni V.L., 3rd Egg Consumption in Infants is Associated with Longer Recumbent Length and Greater Intake of Several Nutrients Essential in Growth and Development. Nutrients. 2018;10:719. doi: 10.3390/nu10060719.","entailment":"The citation’s title reports an association between infant egg consumption and both longer recumbent length and higher nutrient intake, which underpins the claim.","population":"infants","intervention":"egg consumption in infants","comparator":"","outcome":"longer recumbent length and greater intake of several nutrients essential in growth and development","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Infant egg consumption during introduction to solid food remains low in the UK but increases with infant age and a baby led weaning approach.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"- 129.Rowan H., Brown A. Infant egg consumption during introduction to solid food remains low in the UK but increases with infant age and a baby led weaning approach. J. Hum. Nutr. Diet. 2023 doi: 10.1111/jhn.13140.","entailment":"The citation’s title directly states that egg consumption is low initially but rises as infants get older and when a baby‑led weaning method is used, supporting the claim.","population":"infants in the UK","intervention":"baby led weaning approach","comparator":"","outcome":"egg consumption during introduction to solid food","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"}]}
